data.title: Here's How Much $100 Invested In FTAI Aviation 10 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
data.description:

data.title: What's Going On With The Gain In Dell Stock?
data.description:
Dell Technologies Inc (NYSE:DELL) shares are trading higher on Thursday, standing out in a market that's broadly under pressure. Dell is moving in the opposite direction of the Nasdaq, which is down 0.52% and the S&P 500 is off 0.37%.

Despite a broader market downturn, Dell’s stock is bucking the trend, with the Nasdaq down 0.52% and the S&P 500 shedding 0.37%.

  • Dell Technologies shares are trending higher. Why is DELL stock trading higher?

Dell Outperforms Amid Market Downturn

Analyst Upgrade Boosts Sentiment Ahead Of Earnings

Additionally, Evercore ISI raised its rating to Tactical Outperform, signaling confidence heading into Dell's upcoming earnings release, according to CNBC. The firm did trim its price target to $160 from $180, but said it expects Dell to beat current revenue and EPS estimates of $31.4 billion and $3.52.

The firm also noted that rising memory prices are actually helping Dell in the short term. Concerns about memory inflation are prompting customers to accelerate purchases of PCs and traditional servers to get ahead of expected price increases. Evercore ISI believes this pull‑forward effect has boosted Dell's recent demand environment.

Dell’s Technical Indicators Show Mixed Signals

Dell’s stock is currently trading at $118.50, showing a modest gain of 0.4% above its 20-day SMA but remains 11.5% below its 100-day SMA. Over the past 12 months, the stock has seen a slight decline of 2.31%. It is positioned closer to its 52-week low of $66.25 than its high of $168.08, indicating some potential room for recovery or further retraction depending on upcoming market conditions.

The RSI stands at 46.73, suggesting a neutral market momentum, while the MACD indicates a bullish signal with a histogram value of 17 cents. These indicators suggest that while the stock is not currently in overbought or oversold territory, there is potential for upward movement if market conditions improve.

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $163.71. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Morgan Stanley: Underweight (Lowers Target to $101.00) (Feb. 18)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Lowers Target to $160.00) (Feb. 17)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Lowers Target to $165.00) (Jan. 20)

Dell’s Mixed Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Dell Technologies, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Neutral (Score: 39.4) — Reflects a fair valuation compared to industry peers.
  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 20.44) — Indicates that the stock is underperforming the broader market momentum.

The Verdict: Dell’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals mixed signals. The moderate value score suggests a balanced investment at current levels, while the weak momentum score indicates potential challenges ahead. Investors should monitor these trends closely, especially with earnings on the ho

DELL Price Action: Dell shares were up 1.06% at $117.98 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Demystifying Cardinal Infrastructure: Insights From 4 Analyst Reviews
data.description:
In the latest quarter, 4 analysts provided ratings for Cardinal Infrastructure (NASDAQ:CDNL), showcasing a mix of bullish and bearish perspectives.

In the table below, you'll find a summary of their recent ratings, revealing the shifting sentiments over the past 30 days and comparing them to the previous months.

The 12-month price targets, analyzed by analysts, offer insights with an average target of $31.0, a high estimate of $35.00, and a low estimate of $28.00. Observing a 6.9% increase, the current average has risen from the previous average price target of $29.00.

Analyzing Analyst Ratings: A Detailed Breakdown

The perception of Cardinal Infrastructure by financial experts is analyzed through recent analyst actions. The following summary presents key analysts, their recent evaluations, and adjustments to ratings and price targets.

Key Insights:

Assessing these analyst evaluations alongside crucial financial indicators can provide a comprehensive overview of Cardinal Infrastructure's market position. Stay informed and make well-judged decisions with the assistance of our Ratings Table.

Stay up to date on Cardinal Infrastructure analyst ratings.

If you are interested in following small-cap stock news and performance you can start by tracking it here.

Discovering Cardinal Infrastructure: A Closer Look

Cardinal Infrastructure Group Inc provides a comprehensive suite of infrastructure services to the residential, commercial, industrial, municipal, and state infrastructure markets. Its operations leverage a large highly skilled workforce and a fleet of specialized equipment to deliver wet utility installations (water, sewer, and stormwater systems), as well as grading, site clearing, erosion control, drilling and blasting, paving, and other related site services.

Cardinal Infrastructure's Financial Performance

Market Capitalization: With restricted market capitalization, the company is positioned below industry averages. This reflects a smaller scale relative to peers.

Revenue Growth: Cardinal Infrastructure displayed positive results in 3M. As of 30 September, 2025, the company achieved a solid revenue growth rate of approximately 60.22%. This indicates a notable increase in the company's top-line earnings. As compared to competitors, the company surpassed expectations with a growth rate higher than the average among peers in the Industrials sector.

Net Margin: Cardinal Infrastructure's net margin falls below industry averages, indicating challenges in achieving strong profitability. With a net margin of -2.82%, the company may face hurdles in effective cost management.

Return on Equity (ROE): Cardinal Infrastructure's ROE lags behind industry averages, suggesting challenges in maximizing returns on equity capital. With an ROE of -11.75%, the company may face hurdles in achieving optimal financial performance.

Return on Assets (ROA): Cardinal Infrastructure's ROA is below industry averages, indicating potential challenges in efficiently utilizing assets. With an ROA of -1.34%, the company may face hurdles in achieving optimal financial returns.

Debt Management: Cardinal Infrastructure's debt-to-equity ratio stands notably higher than the industry average, reaching 2.43. This indicates a heavier reliance on borrowed funds, raising concerns about financial leverage.

Analyst Ratings: Simplified

Experts in banking and financial systems, analysts specialize in reporting for specific stocks or defined sectors. Their comprehensive research involves attending company conference calls and meetings, analyzing financial statements, and engaging with insiders to generate what are known as analyst ratings for stocks. Typically, analysts assess and rate each stock once per quarter.

Some analysts publish their predictions for metrics such as growth estimates, earnings, and revenue to provide additional guidance with their ratings. When using analyst ratings, it is important to keep in mind that stock and sector analysts are also human and are only offering their opinions to investors.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.

data.title: 3 Retail Stocks That May Gain From Trump's 2026 Tax Refunds
data.description:
Bigger tax refunds are landing in Americans' bank accounts, and fresh stimulus from President Donald Trump's 2026 tax changes could translate into a tangible boost in retail spending.

An 11% increase in the average refund may look modest on paper. Yet for households facing tight budgets, that extra cash can drive incremental purchases.

IRS data through Feb. 6 shows the average individual refund rose to $2,290 in 2026 from $2,065 in 2025. Analysts expect that momentum to build through late February and March.

Refund Momentum Is Building

In a note shared this week, Bank of America analyst Lorraine Hutchinson, CFA, expects the 2026 refund increase versus 2025 to widen as the tax season progresses.

A key catalyst is now looming. Filers who claimed the Earned Income Tax Credit and the Additional Child Tax Credit cannot have refunds processed until mid-February due to a 2015 ruling.

That sets up the potential for a noticeable jump in reported refund totals in the coming weeks.

Beyond timing effects, structural changes are also at play.

Provisions from last year's One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) are expected to deliver approximately $1,000 of stimulus per household, on average, during tax season, according to economists at Bank of America.

Measures such as a higher SALT deduction cap and the "no tax on overtime" provision are contributing to both larger refunds and smaller payments owed.

Historically, refunds peak in February and remain elevated through March and April — aligning closely with key spring retail periods.

Why This Matters for Retail

Lower and middle-income consumers tend to spend refunds rather than save them.

Early data suggests many households track those payments closely. Visits to IRS.gov climbed 35% year over year as of Feb. 6.

Last year, clothing was the largest beneficiary of tax refund spending among low-income households.

“We think this means retailers serving low/middle income consumers will see the largest lift in 2026, though stretched budgets from tariff-driven inflation means dollars are likely to be directed toward essentials first, with only the remainder flowing into discretionary categories,” Hutchinson said.

For investors, the key question isn't just how much money is coming back to households — it's where those dollars will ultimately land.

If history is any guide, value-oriented retailers could be among the first to feel the positive impact.

3 Retail Stocks In Focus

Bank of America highlighted three potential stock winners in the consumer retailer space.

Image via Shutterstock/ noamgalai

data.title: Joby Stock Shows Death Cross — But Pentagon Hypersonic Deal Changes Narrative
data.description:
Joby Aviation Inc (NYSE:JOBY) shares have entered a confirmed long-term downtrend, with the stock flashing a Death Cross even as new Pentagon funding places the company inside the U.S. hypersonic technology ecosystem.

Chart created using Benzinga Pro

JOBY stock is trading near $9.99, down about 30% year-to-date and roughly 52% below its 52-week high of $20.95.

The recent technical breakdown pushed the company's 50-day moving average to $13.28, falling below the 200-day moving average at $13.42 — confirming a Death Cross, a widely followed signal of long-term trend weakness.

Stock Remains Below Key Trend Levels

Joby is now trading approximately 25% below its 50-day moving average and about 26% below its 200-day moving average. The stock has declined more than 32% over the past month, reflecting sustained selling pressure.

Momentum indicators also remain weak. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is currently near 33, a level associated with reduced buying activity and persistent downward momentum. Trading volume has averaged about 4.0 million shares in recent sessions, reflecting continued investor repositioning.

Pentagon Program Expands Defense Exposure

The company has also partnered with L3Harris Technologies Inc (NYSE:LHX) to explore defense applications for its autonomous aircraft platform, expanding its potential role beyond commercial air mobility.

Manufacturing Expansion Continues As JOBY Stock Tests Resistance

Joby recently agreed to acquire a manufacturing facility in Dayton, Ohio, spanning more than 700,000 square feet. The facility is expected to support plans to increase production capacity to four aircraft per month beginning in 2027.

However, the stock remains well below its 200-day moving average, a key long-term trend level that often determines broader directional momentum.

Photo: ARVD73 / Shutterstock.com
